basis: Anchor 30 plan lists Miirym as a GUR Dragon typal/copy Commander anchor.
EDHREC and public Commander deck pages label Miirym as a Commander deck commander.
cEDH relevance is not treated as proven; this profile is casual/high-power Dragon copy guidance.

source_limit_notes: Aggregate/manual reference only; no copied public decklist.
Dragon copy/ETB packages are prioritized over generic Temur goodstuff.
No mainstream cEDH default is asserted for this commander.

themes.name: dragon_typal_copy
themes.notes: Miirym doubles nontoken Dragons, making every Dragon threat also a token-value engine.

themes.name: temur_ramp_battlecruiser
themes.notes: High ramp density is required to cast Dragon threats before the table stabilizes.

themes.name: etb_damage_payoffs
themes.notes: Terror of the Peaks style effects convert copied Dragons into immediate damage.

examples: too many six-plus mana Dragons without ramp or ETB payoff
avoid_patterns.reason: Miirym needs ramp and copy/ETB synergy, not only expensive threats.

examples: clone package with no legend-safe support
avoid_patterns.reason: Clone packages must work with legendary permanents and token-copy rules.
Commander relevance is proven; cEDH default relevance is not proven.
